name: Hadoop 1.x (HDFS & MapReduce v1) Architecture
description: Hadoop 1.x is the foundational architecture of the Apache Hadoop project, following a strictly Master-Slave model. It is composed of two primary layers: HDFS for storage and MapReduce v1 for processing. Unlike modern versions (YARN), the 1.x architecture uses a centralized JobTracker to handle both resource management and task scheduling, which often became a bottleneck. The NameNode manages the file system namespace and metadata, representing a Single Point of Failure (SPOF) in this version. The Secondary NameNode assists by merging edit logs with the FsImage but does not provide high availability.
